L1.area: Git repository data retrieval and local file storage logic

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where remotely-controlled file path values from a Git repository's metadata are used without sanitization to construct local file system paths. An attacker can craft a malicious .git directory with specially crafted path references containing directory traversal sequences, allowing arbitrary file writes outside the intended output directory and potentially enabling remote code execution.

L3.area: Git object and pack file download and local persistence routines
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where file path values extracted from a remote Git repository's pack index metadata are directly interpolated into local file system paths without sanitization. When reconstructing a .git directory from a remote server, an attacker can provide crafted path values containing directory traversal sequences in pack file references and other downloaded file paths, allowing writes outside the intended output directory and enabling arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The downloadFile and parsePackMetadata functions construct local file paths by directly concatenating unsanitized remote path values, allowing an attacker to use directory traversal sequences to write files outside the intended output directory.
